backlog.title: Decide whether Control UI will ever sit behind a reverse proxy
backlog.description: If exposed through a reverse proxy later, set gateway.trustedProxies explicitly. If it stays local-only on loopback, this warning is acceptable.

inProgress.title: Finding app ideas
inProgress.description: Find successful apps in Windows Store that do not exist in Apple Store.

notes.text: Warren: Focus on desktop-control and workflow utilities with recurring pain. Best categories: clipboard/text workflow, file workflow, audio/device switching. Avoid cosmetic-only utilities and broad consumer apps with weak conversion.

Elon: Fastest credible path is a 48-hour sprint: scrape 200-500 Windows candidates across transferable categories, score by reviews/ratings/polish, narrow to 50-75, manually verify Apple absence, then produce a top-10 list with MVP difficulty and monetization angle.

Mario: Don’t just look for anything missing on Apple. Hunt for neglected but valuable workflows. Most interesting gaps are workflow utilities, desktop control/customization, prosumer niche tools, and light enterprise edge cases. Build a shortlist of 20, then score top 5 deeply.
Barry Bot: deduplicated three duplicate backlog cards into this single canonical task and moved it to In Progress.

complete.title: Correct ineffective denyCommands entries
complete.description: Removed invalid gateway.nodes.denyCommands entries that were not actually enforceable.

complete.title: Tag Mission Control cards by executive lens
complete.description: Mission Control now visibly shows whether cards belong to Mario, Elon, Warren, Barry Bot, or Sim.

complete.title: Create a weekly exec review ritual

complete.title: Define business operating structure
complete.description: Created the org structure, decision framework, and Kanban operating model for Sim, Mario, Elon, Warren, and Barry Bot.
